commands.review_summary:
The PR makes `sessions_spawn` ACP argument redaction idempotent for already-redacted `resumeSessionId` and `streamTo` fields and adds a focused sanitizer reference-equality regression test.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +27. Total +27 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main deterministically returns a new object for already-redacted ACP fields, and the replay predicate rejects changed tool-call block references. I did not run the live ACP/Anthropic reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is human merge handling for real behavior proof or an override; there is no narrow code repair indicated.

target.title: fix(agents): make sessions_spawn ACP arg redaction idempotent
target.body:
## Summary

`redactSessionsSpawnAcpArgs` always creates a new object when `resumeSessionId` or `streamTo` is present — even when the value is already `__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__`. This breaks the `sanitizeToolCallBlock(block) !== block` reference-equality check in `isReplaySafeThinkingAssistantTurn`, causing thinking+`sessions_spawn` turns to be incorrectly classified as unsafe and dropped from the LLM's replay context. Effect: `streamTo: "parent"` can be lost on extended-thinking sessions (Anthropic models with `allowProviderOwnedThinkingReplay`).

Root cause traced in #81128 by @kostas97sab5-max.

## Changes

- `src/agents/session-transcript-repair.ts`: skip re-redacting fields already set to `REDACTED_SESSIONS_SPAWN_ATTACHMENT_CONTENT` — guards with `rec[key] !== REDACTED_SESSIONS_SPAWN_ATTACHMENT_CONTENT` before assignment so already-redacted blocks return the original reference
- `src/agents/session-transcript-repair.attachments.test.ts`: regression guard asserting `sanitizeToolCallInputs(alreadyRedacted)` returns the original reference (idempotency on second pass)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `sanitizeToolCallInputs` called on already-redacted `sessions_spawn` blocks now returns the original array reference instead of creating a new one, preserving reference equality through `isReplaySafeThinkingAssistantTurn`.
- **Real environment tested**: WSL2 Ubuntu-24.04, upstream clone `openclaw/openclaw`, branch `fix/sessions-spawn-redaction-idempotency`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-transcript-repair.attachments.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-transcript-repair.attachments.test.ts

   ✓ sanitizeToolCallInputs redacts sessions_spawn attachments > replaces attachments[].content with __OPENCLAW_REDACTED__ 6ms
   ✓ sanitizeToolCallInputs redacts sessions_spawn attachments > redacts attachments content from tool input payloads too 1ms
   ✓ sanitizeToolCallInputs redacts sessions_spawn attachments > replaces non-content attachment payload fields with a minimal redacted stub 1ms
   ✓ sanitizeToolCallInputs redacts sessions_spawn attachments > redacts ACP-only routing fields from arguments and input payloads 1ms
   ✓ sanitizeToolCallInputs redacts sessions_spawn attachments > redacts ACP-only routing fields with non-string payloads 0ms
   ✓ sanitizeToolCallInputs redacts sessions_spawn attachments > re-sanitizing already-redacted ACP fields returns the original reference 0ms

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  6 passed (6)
     Duration  439ms
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: New test "re-sanitizing already-redacted ACP fields returns the original reference" passes; without the fix it fails because `sanitizeToolCallInputs` returns a new array (reference inequality).
- **What was not tested**: A live Anthropic API replay session with `streamTo: "parent"` and `allowProviderOwnedThinkingReplay` active. See note below on what that proof would add.

**Why the unit test is the direct proof of this predicate:**

`isReplaySafeThinkingAssistantTurn` (`src/agents/session-transcript-repair.ts:185-211`) is the function that decides whether a thinking+`sessions_spawn` assistant turn is preserved or dropped on replay. It returns `false` (drops the turn) whenever any tool call block fails the check at line 206:

```ts
if (sanitizeToolCallBlock(block) !== block) {
  return false;
}
```

Before this fix, an already-redacted `sessions_spawn` block always produced a new object from `redactSessionsSpawnAcpArgs` → `sanitizeToolCallBlock` returned a new reference → `!== block` was true → turn dropped.

After this fix, `redactSessionsSpawnAcpArgs` detects already-redacted fields and returns the original object unchanged → `sanitizeToolCallBlock` returns the same reference → `!== block` is false → turn preserved.

The regression test `re-sanitizing already-redacted ACP fields returns the original reference` asserts this reference equality directly, on the exact code path that `isReplaySafeThinkingAssistantTurn` exercises. It fails on unpatched main and passes on this PR head.

A live Anthropic API proof with `allowProviderOwnedThinkingReplay` (enabled at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts:677`) would add confidence in the broader replay plumbing and provider-shaped content handling. It would not change what this unit test proves about the local sanitizer/predicate reference-equality condition: the `!== block` check at `:206` is a property of the sanitizer itself, exercised directly by the regression test.

Closes #81128
target.author: martingarramon
